{{Quote|Please enjoy the fabulous ''Lucky Despot''.|[[Sneg]] the Hand, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:StH">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=conversation|Sneg the Hand|Tatooine}}</ref>}}
The '''''Lucky Despot''''' was a large cargo ship which became a notable hotel/casino in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] owned and operated by [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]].

==History==
{{Quote|Greetings and be welcome. Please enjoy the facilities here at the Lucky Despot. Are you here to try your luck at one of our gaming tables or....perhaps you are here for business rather then pleasure?|Sneg the Hand, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:StH" />}}
[[File:LuckyDespotMap.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Floor plans of the ''Lucky Despot'']]
[[File:Lucky_Despot_Hotel.jpg|200px|thumb|Another view of the ''Lucky Despot'']]
The ''Lucky Despot'' once flew a shipment of [[Mini-Huvicko/Yuzabi Dowser binary hydromech droid|M-HYD hydromech droids]] to the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where its captain hoped to market the droids to moisture prospectors in the [[Jundland Wastes/Legends|Jundland Wastes]]. The ship remained in Mos Eisley after the captain lost all his money in a disastrous lawsuit following a clash with disgruntled M-HYD customers.

Later, the ''Lucky Despot'' was converted into a hotel because it was no longer capable of flight. A consortium of offworlders purchased the hulk and towed it to a new location, half-buried it in dirt, and rebuilt the interior. The new hotel opened shortly before [[Boonta Eve/Legends|Boonta's Eve]]. It made a profit for a decade, falling into bankruptcy and closure with the decline of [[Podracing/Legends|podracing]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and the arrival of largely criminal patronage.

Not long after the rise of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the hotel was purchased by Valarian, who swiftly found that her new investment was unable to turn a profit. Bureaucratic inertia and vandalism prevented success. She traced her problems to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], and offered him a small percentage of it. A compromise was reached, and the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] agreed to limit her criminal activities outside of Mos Eisley. He tipped Lady Valarian off to Imperial gambling raids, for a price.<ref name="GG7" />

The ''Lucky Despot'' was the scene of the wedding reception between Valarian and her husband, [[D'Wopp]]. Many citizens of Mos Eisley were in attendance, including [[Wuher/Legends|Wuher]] and [[Kardue'sai'Malloc/Legends|Labria]], with music provided by the [[Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes/Legends|Modal Nodes]]. A fight between the bride and groom broke out, and Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] arrived, presumably tipped off by Jabba, to break up an illegal gambling operation.

The [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]] [[Sneg]] worked in the ''Lucky Despot'' after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], buying any kind of second-hand weapons for the use of the [[Valarian Syndicate|Valarian gang]].<ref name="SWG:StH" /> The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Garden/Legends|gardener]] [[Kardeer]] also worked there.<ref name="SWG:K">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=conversation|Kardeer|Tatooine}}</ref>

{{Loc-stub}}
